relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Factionalism shows the community still moving as flesh-patterned infants.
Milk came before solid food because capacity was not ready.
Jealousy and strife reveal human-patterned walking.
Paul and Apollos faction-names expose immature identity.
Leaders are servants through whom trust came.
One plants, one waters, but the Divine grows.
The growth-giving Divine is the center, not the worker.
Planting and watering are one labor under the Divine.
The community is Divine field and Divine building.
Grace lays foundation, but each must watch how they build.
Jesus Messiah is the only foundation.
Different materials reveal different kinds of building.
Fire unveils the quality of each work.
What remains receives reward.
What burns brings loss, yet saving/healing may pass through fire.
The community is Divine temple, indwelt by Spirit.
To damage the community-temple is holy danger.
Age-wisdom must become foolish to become truly wise.
World-wisdom is caught in its own craftiness.
The Lord knows empty reasonings.
Boasting in humans collapses because all gifts belong to the body.
Leaders, world, life, death, present and future all serve the larger belonging.
You belong to Messiah, and Messiah belongs to the Divine.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 3 reframes community as field, building, and temple. Division is not just disagreement; it harms the sacred body where Spirit dwells.
